channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Residential Proxies for Web Automation: What We Learned Running 500+ Accounts
item.description: If you're building automation for platforms with anti-abuse systems, proxy infrastructure is the unglamorous foundation that determines whether your system works or gets every account flagged. At...

item.title: Protecting GitHub from Supply-Chain Malware: Prevention, Cleanup, and Recovery
item.description: GitHub is not just a place where code lives. In most engineering organizations, it is part of the software delivery control plane. That means a compromised developer machine, OAuth app, GitHub...

item.title: Built a CSS Framework Around an Idea Most Had Forgotten About
item.description: CSS has always had a clear purpose: describe how a document looks, not what it contains. Somewhere along the way, frameworks lost sight of that. Utility frameworks traded readable markup for...

item.title: Nodejs : from Hate to love
item.description: "simplicity is the ultimate sophistication" by "leonardo da vinci".To understand it I spend a lot of my precious time. I was the guy who was curious about the technology when nobody around me...

item.title: The Journey Begins: Week 1 as an Aspiring Data Professional.
item.description: Introduction ‎Ladies and gentlemen, I believe in this era of social media, we have all come across content that encourages us to 'Awaken the beast within'. Up until a week ago, I thought that it...

item.title: Built ScratchCode – A Platform That Makes Learning Code Visual
item.description: Most coding platforms teach syntax. Very few help beginners understand what actually happens when code runs. As an IT student, I noticed that many learners struggle with concepts like variables,...

item.title: Understanding Attention in Transformers — Intuition Before Equations
item.description: When people first hear about Transformers, they often encounter words like Query, Key, Value, and Attention Heads and feel confused. But the main idea of attention is actually simple. Attention...

item.title: Event Sourcing Is Just Reduce Over Time
item.description: Event Sourcing has a reputation problem. The moment someone mentions it, the conversation usually becomes: Event Store CQRS Snapshots Replay Projections Distributed Systems And most developers...

item.title: I was hired to manage the code, not write it. I left as their CTO.
item.description: The first thing I was hired to do wasn't development. "Just check whether our code is healthy." Read the code, point out what needed fixing, file the bugs, track them. Code management, basically....

item.title: I Launched My Analytics Tool 3 Months Ago. It Got 1 Upvote. Here's What I Rebuilt.
item.description: The Original Problem (Still Valid) Google Analytics sends every user interaction to Google's servers. Every click, every session, every IP address — all of it leaves your infrastructure. For...

item.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
item.description: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.

item.title: Five labs, five minds: building a multi-model finance drama on small models
What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: The first Story-Rich showcase was packed with narrative-driven games
item.description: Fellow Traveller, the publisher behind games like Titanium Court and 1000xResist, just wrapped up its Story-Rich Showcase, which featured a bunch of narrative-driven indie games. With more than 20...

item.title: GOG apologizes for emailing people Nazi symbols
item.description: GOG sent a newsletter about the game The End of the Sun on June 5th that included symbols associated with the Nazi SS. The Steam competitor issued a statement attributing the inclusion to a...

item.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
item.description: Krishnan is reportedly starting a new institution to continue shaping Trump's AI policy.

item.title: The cutest games from the Wholesome Direct 2026 showcase
item.description: Every year at Summer Game Fest, nestled in between the splashy blockbuster showcases, the Wholesome Direct provides a nice change of pace. It's similarly packed with games - this year's edition...

item.title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
item.description: President Donald Trump said he's discussing deals "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI."

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026: All the news from gaming’s busiest week
item.description: Get ready for some gaming news. It’s officially June, which means splashy new events from PlayStation, Xbox, gaming hype man Geoff Keighley, and more. PlayStation kicked things off with a big...

item.title: 4K Blu-rays are three for $33 ahead of Father’s Day
item.description: If you know a dad with a predilection for physical media, he might really dig what you buy for him with this deal at Gruv. Ahead of Father’s Day on June 21st, the online seller (operated by...

item.title: The mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, says only people who live in ‘shitty houses’ oppose data center
item.description: A proposed $2 billion data center has become a political flashpoint in the small city of Shelbyville, Indiana. And the controversy has only grown more intense after the mayor, Scott Furgeson, was...

item.title: Benn Jordan longs for the days of tech that didn’t spy on you
item.description: Benn Jordan may have initially gained notoriety for his music as Flashbulb and later, reviewing synths and effects pedals on YouTube under Benn and Gear. But about five years ago, Benn decided to...

item.title: 82-0 is the best basketball game, to hell with NBA 2K
item.description: 82-0 marries the stat nerd fun of fantasy basketball with instant gratification and a bit of dumb luck. The goal is to draft a team of players that could (theoretically) have a perfect 82-0...

item.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
item.description: Facebook has long been filled with feeds of clickbait articles. Now, Meta is making its own clickbait articles with AI. The standalone Meta AI app now has a "For You" section that populates a list...

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description: OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks. The feature is primarily...

item.title: Kabuto Park captures the fleeting joy of summer vacation
item.description: There are a lot of games that remind me of summer - hot days in the backseat with a copy of Dragon Warrior III, cooling off in the basement while grinding Gran Turismo races - but there aren't a...

item.title: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
item.description: Plus: Hackers use Meta’s AI bots to hack Instagram accounts, Anthropic helps NSA hackers, a decades-long GPS satellite mystery may have been solved, and more.

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description: A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited...

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description: Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video,...
